---
title: "Rethinking neglected tropical disease prevalence survey design and analysis: a geospatial paradigm"
subtitle: "Transactions of the Royal Society of Tropical Medicine and Hygiene 115(3):208–210 (2021)"
date: 2021-03-06
doi: 10.1093/trstmh/trab020
authors:
  - Peter J. Diggle
  - Benjamin Amoah
  - Claudio Fronterre
  - Emanuele Giorgi
  - Olatunji Johnson
categories: ["Journal article"]
---

**Links:** [Publisher (DOI)](https://doi.org/10.1093/trstmh/trab020)

**Summary.** Advocates for a shift from design-based to model-based geostatistical approaches in NTD prevalence surveys, arguing that spatial modelling delivers more precise prevalence estimates, supports adaptive sampling strategies, and is better suited to the needs of elimination programmes.
